headline: Surgeon's Obstetric Fistula Work Wins UN Population Award
meta_description: A Senegalese surgeon dedicated to restoring women's health and dignity, along with a Burundian foundation, receive the 2026 UN Population Award.
summary_short: A Senegalese surgeon who restores health to women with obstetric fistula has won the prestigious 2026 UN Population Award alongside a Burundian foundation.
body:
Recognition has come to a Senegalese surgeon whose life's work centers on repairing obstetric fistula, a devastating condition that affects women in vulnerable circumstances. The surgeon's career has focused on restoring not only physical health but also the dignity and social reintegration of patients. This commitment earned him the 2026 United Nations Population Award, one of the world's most respected honors in reproductive health.

Sharing the award is a Burundian foundation dedicated to supporting women and children facing extreme hardship. Together, the honorees represent the quiet, steady work of healing and advocacy that transforms individual lives and strengthens entire communities. Their recognition underscores the global importance of accessible reproductive healthcare and the profound human impact of those who dedicate themselves to this mission.
